steps.n: These are two separate words that work together - عَنْ means 'about' and مَا means 'what'

steps.m: about what (asking about يَتَسَآءَلُونَ - they are asking)
steps.n: When عَنْ and مَا come together, they combine into one word. The ن and م merge to make مّ (with a shadda). This is a common way to make Arabic flow better when speaking.
The doubling of the middle letter changes this from a general concept to a specific family relation
This is the basic meaning of asking questions
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif makes it about asking each other, not just asking
Added تَ prefix to show people doing action to each other
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending to ُ - means 'he asks others and they ask him back'
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The word is asking about عَمَّ (about what)
This is a basic preposition that shows what something is about or concerning. It connects to the word ٱلنَّبَإِ (the news/information) in this ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلنَّبَإِ (the news/information)
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (telling news) to a noun (news) that comes after the word عَنِ
Adding ال makes the word definite, like adding 'the' in English
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is the object of عَنِ (about/concerning)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
The ي between the second and third root letters makes this an intensive adjective, showing something is very great or magnificent

steps.m: the Great One (describing ٱلنَّبَإِ - the news)
steps.n: The word 'great' gets 'the' added at the start and matches the ending of 'the news' that it's describing
This is a relative pronoun used to connect clauses and describe something mentioned before
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to the people who are disagreeing (مُخْتَلِفُونَ) about something.
This is a basic preposition showing location or state. It connects to the word مُخْتَلِفُونَ (differing) to show what they are differing in.

steps.m: in it (referring back to something previously mentioned)
steps.n: The هِ at the end refers back to something masculine mentioned earlier in the conversation. This creates a complete prepositional phrase.

steps.m: to differ, to disagree, to vary
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root letters
Added letters show people doing the action back and forth with each other (like arguing)
Changed the verb into a describing word - like changing 'disagree' to 'disagreeing'
The ون ending shows this describes multiple people (they). This word describes what 'they' are doing regarding الَّذِى (that which/what)
This is the basic form of the word used to say no or deny something
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) makes the denial stronger

steps.m: No indeed! (responding to what comes after: سَيَعْلَمُونَ - they will know)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end makes it a complete statement of rejection. This word strongly denies what follows - that they will know the truth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adding ون shows this is about multiple people (they) rather than just one person (he)
Added سَ at the start to make it future tense
This word connects ideas by showing that one thing happens after another. Here it connects to the next word سَيَعْلَمُونَ (they will know) to show a sequence.
This is the basic form of the word that means to stop or prevent something
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: no indeed, absolutely not! (shows strong denial or rejection of what was mentioned before ثُمَّ)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end turns this into a special word used to strongly disagree with or correct wrong ideas. Here it's rejecting whatever was mentioned after ثُمَّ (then)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing vowels
Changed from singular to plural by adding ون at the end
Added سَ at the start to show this will happen in the future
Added مْ to make it negative. This changes it from meaning 'for/to' to meaning 'not' for past actions.
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question. When أَ is added to a negative statement, it expects the answer 'yes'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added kasra at end to show this is affected by a word that came before it
This refers to the physical ground or planet we live on
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the basic noun pattern
Added ال to make it definite ('the earth') and fatḥa at the end shows it's receiving the action of the verb نَجْعَلِ (we made)
The basic idea of making something flat and comfortable

steps.m: a smooth spread-out place (object of نَجْعَلِ)
steps.n: Changed from action to place. This word describes what Allah made the earth into - a comfortable spread-out place.

steps.m: a smooth spread-out place (2nd object of نَجْعَلِ - what the earth was made into)
steps.n: Added an alif at the end to show this is what the earth was made into (like saying 'We made the earth a bed'). The alif looks different (ٰ) just because of writing style.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed first vowel to kasra and added alif to show many mountains
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and the mountains (object of an implied verb 'made')
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by an implied verb جعل (made)
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make the noun for 'peg'
The word changes from singular to plural by adding أَ at the start and ا in the middle, like how 'peg' becomes 'pegs'
The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon. In this ayah, it's the object of an understood verb meaning 'We made' - so the full meaning is 'And We made the mountains as pegs'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نا (we) to خلق (created) to make it 'we created' instead of 'he created'. The نا is written as نٰ with a line on top in this style of writing.
Added كُمْ to show who was created. The sukun (ْ) on م shows the end of the word.

steps.m: and we created you all (as pairs/mates)
steps.n: Added و with fatha (وَ) at start to connect this to what came before. This introduces the statement about Allah creating humans as pairs.

steps.m: to join things together in pairs

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun meaning one pair or spouse
Added أ at the beginning and ا after و to make it plural, showing multiple pairs or spouses

steps.m: as pairs/spouses (object of خَلَقْنٰ - We created)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb خَلَقْنٰ (We created) - like saying 'We created you AS pairs'

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نَا changes it from 'he' to 'we'. This word is the main verb acting on نَوْمَكُمْ (your sleep) which it makes into سُبَاتًا (a rest).
Adding وَ at the start means 'and'
Changed the verb (to sleep) into a noun (sleep) by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This is like changing 'to sleep' to 'sleep' in English.
Added كُمْ at the end to show the sleep belongs to multiple people being spoken to. This word is the object (thing being affected) by جَعَلْنَا (We made) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that describes the state of complete rest by adding damma at start and alif before final letter

steps.m: as a deep rest (object of جَعَلْنَا)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of جَعَلْنَا (We made). The full meaning in context is 'We made your sleep [into] a deep rest.'

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: we made (the night into a covering)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is followed by الَّيْلَ (the night) and لِبَاسًا (covering) to complete the meaning.

steps.m: and we made (the night into a covering)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'night'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the ل with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلْنَا (we made)
Changed to noun pattern that means the thing used for covering. This word is what Allah says He made the night into - 'We made the night a covering.'
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object receiving the action of 'making' in the phrase 'We made the night a covering'

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb form. This shows the action was completed.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is connected to النَّهَارَ مَعَاشًا (the day [as] livelihood) which tells what we made.

steps.m: and we made/put (subject of النَّهَارَ مَعَاشًا)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the previous ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The verb 'to flow brightly' becomes the noun 'daytime'. Like how flowing water becomes a river, the flowing of light becomes the time we call day. It's the thing being 'made' in the sentence.

steps.m: the daytime (object of جَعَلْنَا - we made)
steps.n: Added ٱل for 'the' and َ at the end because it's the object of جَعَلْنَا (we made)

steps.m: to live, exist, earn a living

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added مَ prefix to create a noun meaning the place or means of the action
Added ًا to show this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'We made the day a livelihood'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and added an alif at the end because ي changes to ى when it's at the end of a past tense verb
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'he built' to 'we built' - this نا shows that 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: and we built (فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعًا شِدَادًا - above you seven strong [heavens])
steps.n: Adding و at the start means 'and'
Added fatha and sukun to make the noun meaning 'above'. This word appears in وَبَنَيْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ (and We built above you).

steps.m: above you all (object of وَبَنَيْنَا - and We built)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when speaking to multiple people. The whole phrase وَبَنَيْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ means 'and We built above you all'.
Added fatha and sukun to connect the letters and make it a basic number word
The ًا shows this is the object (what was built) in the sentence. Here it refers specifically to seven heavens that Allah built above us.
Root letters got vowels to make an adjective meaning 'strong'
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple strong things
Added tanween alif to show this describes سَبْعًا (seven) in the accusative case
Added vowel marks (َ) to make it mean 'he made/placed' in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The sukun (ْ) is added before نَا to help pronunciation.

steps.m: and we made/placed (connected to what came before)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Changes from verb 'to shine' into noun meaning 'thing that shines'
Added tanween (double vowel mark) to show it's indefinite (a lamp, not the lamp) and to show it's being acted upon. This word is the object of وَجَعَلْنَا (and We made/placed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added shadda on middle letter and alif after it to show something that blazes very strongly
Added tanween fatHa to show this word is describing the lamp (سِرَاجًا) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to cause something to descend' instead of just 'to descend'
Adding نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action

steps.m: and we sent down (water from مِنَ ٱلْمُعْصِرٰتِ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to what came before. This verb acts on مَآءً (water) which comes from مِنَ ٱلْمُعْصِرٰتِ (from the rain clouds)
This is the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects with المعصرات (the rain clouds) to show the rain comes from them.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (المعصرات) which starts with ال. This helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: to squeeze or press out liquid

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who/what does the action

steps.m: rain clouds (literally: those that squeeze out water)
steps.n: Added feminine plural ending to show multiple clouds that squeeze out rain

steps.m: the rain clouds (that squeeze out water)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify these particular rain clouds and ِ at the end because it follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah
The root letters م-و-ه transform into the simple word 'water' (ماء) by changing و to ا and ه to ء. This is a common way Arabic makes basic nouns easier to say.
Adding the long آ makes the word stronger, like saying 'abundant water'

steps.m: water (object of أَنزَلْنَا - We sent down)
steps.n: Adding ً (tanween fatha) shows this is the object receiving the action of 'sending down' in the verse
Connected the root letters and added fatha to show basic past tense meaning
Changes from a verb (he poured) to a descriptive word meaning 'pouring a lot'

steps.m: pouring forth very abundantly (describes مَآءً water)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is describing the indefinite noun مَآءً (water)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding أ at the start makes it mean causing something to go out rather than going out yourself

steps.m: we make (something) go out (حَبًّا وَنَبَاتًا - grain and plants)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with 'we' as the subject. This verb acts on حَبًّا وَنَبَاتًا (grain and plants) in the ayah.

steps.m: so that we may bring out
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something else happens

steps.m: so that we may bring out
steps.n: Added shadda on ل for emphasis and pronunciation
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by means of'

steps.m: with it/by means of it (connected to نُخْرِجَ - we bring forth)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه with kasra (هِ) to show what/who the preposition affects. This whole phrase tells how 'we bring forth' (نُخْرِجَ) is done.

steps.m: with it/by means of it (connected to نُخْرِجَ - we bring forth)
steps.n: Added a small mark (ۦ) after ه to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters ح-ب-ب became the noun حَبّ (grain) by adding a fatha and combining the double ب into one with shadda

steps.m: grain/seed (object of نُخْرِجَ - we bring forth)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is what is being 'brought forth' in the phrase 'we bring forth grain'

steps.m: to sprout or grow (like a plant)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from verb (sprouted) to noun (plant). This word is what is being brought forth by the verb نُخْرِجَ (We bring forth).
Added tanween (ًا) to show this is an indefinite noun being acted upon
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous word حَبًّا (grain)

steps.m: to hide or cover something by enclosing it

steps.n: Added ة to make it a specific garden. The word comes from the idea of an enclosed space with plants.
Changes singular 'garden' to plural 'gardens' by replacing ة with ات
Added ٍ to show this word is being described or connected to something else in the sentence

steps.m: and gardens (connected to أَلْفَافًا - densely growing)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' to connect this word to the description أَلْفَافًا (densely growing) that comes after it

steps.m: to wrap around or grow densely

steps.n: Changes from basic root meaning 'wrap/grow thick' into a word describing thick/tangled plants
This is a broken plural form showing multiple things wrapped together
The ًا ending shows this word is describing the gardens mentioned before it (جَنَّاتٍ). It matches gardens in being indefinite (no ال) and in its grammatical case.
This is the basic form of the word, used to show conditions or possibilities
Adding the shadda changes this from a word meaning 'if' to a word that adds emphasis. Here it emphasizes that the Day of Judgment (يَوْمَ ٱلْفَصْلِ) is definitely appointed.
Connected the root letters together to make the basic noun meaning 'day'. This word is connected to الفَصْلِ (the judgment) to show which day we're talking about.
Added a fatha at the end because إِنَّ (indeed) comes before it and affects it. This is like saying 'Indeed, the day...'

steps.m: to separate or divide things clearly

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added marks to make it mean the act of separation itself. This word belongs to the word يَوْم (day), making the phrase 'Day of Separation'.
Added 'al' at the start to make it specific, like saying 'the separation' instead of just 'separation'
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to the word 'day', making 'Day of Separation'
Added fatha vowels to make the past tense form

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce. This word tells us that the Day of Judgment (يَوْمَ ٱلْفَصْلِ) was appointed as a specific time (مِيقٰتًا).

steps.m: to set or determine a time

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a basic noun
Added مِي at the start to make a special word for when/where something happens. The و changed to ي because it comes after مِ
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of كَانَ (was). The alif is written with a bar on top in this style.
Connected the root letters together. This word shows when the blowing of the horn (يُنفَخُ) happens.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object (answers 'when?')
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the Horn receives the action of blowing. This describes what happens on the Day of Judgment when the Horn is blown to gather people.
This is a basic particle that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows the blowing happens inside the Horn.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to blow, to make a sound (especially of a horn/trumpet)
to be blown (passive voice of horn/trumpet)
steps.n: Changes to passive voice - something is being blown rather than someone blowing
Adding definite article prefix and strengthening the ص with shadda
Adding kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/into) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Added ت at start to make it present tense
Added ون to show multiple men doing the action
Added َ (fatha) to make the word flow better in speech
فَ shows this happens as a result of what came before

steps.m: and then you (all) come (after the trumpet is blown)
steps.n: فَ shows this happens right after the trumpet is blown (mentioned in previous ayah)

steps.m: to move in a group or crowd

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun for a single group
Added أ at beginning and ا after و to make it plural, showing multiple groups

steps.m: in groups (describing how people will come on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action happens. This word explains how people will come (فَتَأْتُونَ) - they will come in groups.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

steps.m: it (feminine) was opened (passive past) (subject is ٱلسَّمَآءُ)
steps.n: Added تِ to match with ٱلسَّمَآءُ (the sky/heaven) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: and it (feminine) was opened (passive past) (subject is ٱلسَّمَآءُ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what came before it

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up

steps.m: the sky (subject of فُتِحَتِ - was opened)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific ('the sky'), doubled the س with shadda for joining sounds, and added ُ to show it's the subject being opened in فُتِحَتِ (was opened)
Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Added ت at the end because السَّمَآءُ (the sky) is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: so it was/then it became (referring to السَّمَآءُ/the sky becoming something with أَبْوٰبًا/gates)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of the sky being opened (وَفُتِحَتِ)
Connected the root letters and added alif to make the basic singular noun form
This is a broken plural form, which means it changes the inside of the word instead of just adding a suffix
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action. This word follows كَانَتْ (it was) in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the mountains are being moved by something else
Added ت to show this is about something feminine (like mountains)

steps.m: and she/it was moved (referring to الْجِبَالُ - the mountains)
steps.n: Added و (and) at beginning to connect with previous statement, and kasra at end because another word follows
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changes the singular 'mountain' into plural form for many mountains
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the mountains' instead of just 'mountains'

steps.m: the mountains (subject of سُيِّرَتِ - were moved)
steps.n: Added damma at the end because 'the mountains' is the subject of the verb 'were moved' in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb with a weak middle letter
Added تْ at the end to show the subject is feminine (the mountains)

steps.m: then they became (referring to the mountains becoming a mirage)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of the mountains being moved (from previous ayah)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: mirage (something that appears to flow/move like water)
steps.n: Changed to noun form meaning the illusion of water seen in the desert that appears to flow or move. This word is used because mirages look like flowing water in the distance.

steps.m: a mirage (describing what the mountains became)
steps.n: Added the tanween (ًا) to show this word is describing what the mountains became in the phrase 'and the mountains became a mirage'
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) was truly lying in wait. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show extra emphasis.
This is a proper noun with no Arabic root. It's believed to come from Hebrew 'Gehinnom', referring to a valley near Jerusalem that became associated with punishment.
Added vowel marks and shadda to show proper pronunciation. This word is the subject of كَانَتْ (was) in this ayah, showing that Hell 'was' something.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (جَهَنَّمَ/hellfire)
This root relates to watching or observing something
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مِ at start and changed vowels to show this is a place where watching happens
Added alif to strengthen the meaning, showing it's not just any watching place but specifically an ambush point

steps.m: a place of watching/ambush (as the predicate of كَانَتْ)
steps.n: Added an 'an' sound at the end to show this word completes the meaning of كَانَتْ (was/were)
Added vowel marks (َ) to make it mean 'he transgressed/exceeded limits' in the past
Changed to describe someone who does this action
Made the word plural to refer to multiple people

steps.m: the ones who go beyond limits
steps.n: Added 'ال' (the) at the start, which made the ط double in sound
Added لِّ (for/to) at the beginning and final vowel َ. This word connects to مَـَٔابًا (a place of return) showing who the place is for.
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens
Added آ to show the meaning of returning or going back

steps.m: a place of return (for الطَّاغِينَ - the transgressors)
steps.n: Added tanween (ًا) at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a place' instead of 'the place'). This word describes what will be for the transgressors mentioned in the previous word (الطَّاغِينَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to describe someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural - changes from 'one who stays' to 'those who stay'

steps.m: surely staying/remaining (describes the people in Hell)
steps.n: Added emphasis with لّ at start and final vowel mark. This word describes the permanent state of people in Hell mentioned in the surrounding verses.

steps.m: in (location word showing where لَّابِثِينَ/remaining happens)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or containment. This word connects to لَّابِثِينَ (remaining) to show where the remaining takes place
Added the kasra vowel mark under ف to show proper pronunciation
Added ها to show we're referring to something feminine (in this case, referring back to Hell/جَهَنَّمَ which is feminine)
The alif is written with a special form (آ) to show a longer, more emphatic pronunciation

steps.m: to stay or remain for a period
a period of time (usually very long)
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning a specific period of time

steps.m: long periods of time (plural of حُقْب)
steps.n: Makes it plural by adding أ at start and ا after second root letter. This is a common pattern for making plurals in Arabic.

steps.m: long periods of time (describing how long they will stay)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word describes how long the action of staying (لَّابِثِينَ) will last. It answers the question 'how long?'
This is a negative particle that prevents the action of tasting from happening

steps.m: absolutely not (strongly negates يَذُوقُونَ - they taste)
steps.n: The shadda and fatha make the negation stronger, showing they definitely won't taste anything
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels makes it present tense

steps.m: they taste (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
steps.n: Adding ونَ makes it plural for 'they'. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers not tasting any coolness or drink in Hell.
This is a basic preposition showing location. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.
Added ها which refers back to Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) which is feminine in Arabic. This word connects to لَّا يَذُوقُونَ (they will not taste) to show what they won't taste in.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. The fatha on ب and sukun on ر show this is a noun meaning the feeling or state of being cold.

steps.m: any coolness (object of يَذُوقُونَ - they taste)
steps.n: Added tanween (ًا) to show this is indefinite (any coolness) and is the object being tasted. This word is what they are not tasting in the phrase 'they will not taste any coolness.'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added لَا which means 'not' to negate what follows. In this ayah, it's negating the word شَرَابًا (drink), saying they will not taste any drink.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from action of drinking to the thing that is drunk. This word is the object (what is being tasted) in the phrase 'they will not taste a drink'.
The ًا ending shows this is the object being acted upon (what they are tasting) and makes it indefinite (any drink rather than a specific drink)
This is the base form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what was mentioned before
The shadda and alif are added to strengthen and complete the exception meaning. In this ayah, it's excluding what follows (hot water and dirty wound discharge) from something mentioned before.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong, intense quality. The word specifically refers to extremely hot or boiling water/liquid.
Added ًا to show this is the object being received. In this ayah, it comes after إِلَّا (except) as part of what the wrongdoers will receive.
The doubled س (shadda) and long ا show this is something that flows continuously and intensely. In this context, it refers to a dark, thick liquid that flows.
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action and matches with حَمِيمًا (boiling water) that came before it - they are both things that will be given.
The و prefix means 'and', connecting this word to what came before it

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he repaid', with ا at the end instead of ي
Changed the verb into a noun that means the result of the action

steps.m: a repayment (as the object being given)
steps.n: Added tanwin (double marks) to show this is indefinite and is being acted upon. The آ is just a spelling variation of ا.

steps.m: to match, to be suitable, to fit together

steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) on first letter and ا (alif) after second letter to create a noun describing the state of being fitting or matching. This word describes جَزَآءً (recompense).

steps.m: as a fitting consequence (describes جَزَآءً - recompense)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to match the word it describes (جَزَآءً/recompense) - like adding 'a' or 'an' in English
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. It emphasizes the verb كَانُوا (they were) in this ayah.
Added the plural masculine pronoun 'them' to the end. This pronoun is the subject of the verb كَانُوا (they were) in this ayah.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb

steps.m: they were (subject of لَا يَرْجُونَ - they do not expect)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change 'he' to 'they'. This word is the subject of لَا يَرْجُونَ (they do not expect) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَرْجُونَ (they hope) into 'they do not hope'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'

steps.m: to hope for or expect something
steps.n: This root is about hoping or expecting something
Basic vowel marks (fatha) added between root letters to make past tense 'he' form
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to damma. The final و stays as و.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to a noun meaning the act of counting or reckoning. This word is the object (accusative case) of the verb يَرْجُونَ (they hope/expect), showing what they did not expect.

steps.m: a reckoning (object of لَا يَرْجُونَ - they were not expecting)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what they were not expecting. In the ayah, this connects to لَا يَرْجُونَ (they were not expecting) to show what they didn't expect.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda to middle root letter creates Form II verb, intensifying the meaning from 'lie' to 'strongly deny/reject'
The وا ending shows that multiple people (they) did the action
The و (and) was added at the start to connect this word to what came before it
Forms the singular noun for something that shows or demonstrates truth
Adds feminine plural suffix -ات

steps.m: our signs (object of كَذَّبُوا - they denied)
steps.n: The نا shows these signs belong to Allah/Us. This word is being denied/rejected by the disbelievers in this ayah.
The بِ shows these signs were the means or instrument of their denial
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha
This form makes a noun that describes someone who does the action a lot - like calling someone a 'big liar'

steps.m: an intense/habitual liar (object of كَذَّبُوا/they denied)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is the object (thing being acted upon) in the sentence
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything with no exceptions. This is the basic word used to talk about all of something.
The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of counting/recording (أَحْصَيْنٰهُ) in this ayah
The و at the start connects this to what came before, showing this is an additional thing being counted/recorded
The root letters ش-ي-أ form the noun شَيْء by adding vowels. It changes from meaning 'to want' to meaning 'a thing'
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end because this word is possessed by كُلَّ (every/all) which came before it. Also changed the ي to ى which is just a writing style change.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: counted completely (like making a detailed list)
steps.n: The added أ at the beginning makes the counting more thorough and complete
Added نا to show 'we' did the counting

steps.m: we counted it completely (referring to كُلَّ شَىْءٍ - everything)
steps.n: The هُ at the end shows what was counted - in this case it refers back to كُلَّ شَىْءٍ (everything) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: a record/writing (object of أَحْصَيْنٰهُ - we have enumerated)
steps.n: This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. In this ayah, كِتٰبًا is in the accusative case because it is the object (what is being recorded) of the verb أَحْصَيْنٰهُ (we have recorded/enumerated).
The ًا ending shows this is the object being acted upon by the verb أَحْصَيْنٰهُ (we have enumerated)
Changes from a basic verb to a command form for a group
Added فَ to show this command is a result or consequence of something mentioned before
This particle connects what comes after it with what came before. Here it connects to فَذُوقُوا۟ (so taste), showing the result or consequence.
Added لَن which is a strong negative particle for future actions. Together with فَ it means 'so never' or 'so will not'. It negates the verb نَّزِيدَكُمْ (We will increase you).
Added vowel marks (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he increased' in the past tense
Adding نَ at the start shows 'we' are doing the action in the present tense
Adding shadda on ن adds emphasis, showing this will definitely happen

steps.m: we will surely increase you all
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows who is being increased (you all)
This is the base form of the exception particle that means 'except'
Adding shadda and alif makes the word stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's saying they will get nothing except more punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This pattern changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what's being increased in the phrase 'We will not increase you except in punishment.'

steps.m: a punishment (object of نَّزِيدَكُمْ - we will increase you)
steps.n: The tanween (double vowel mark) makes it indefinite. This word is the object (accusative case) of the verb نزيد which means 'we will increase'. So Allah is saying He will increase them in nothing except punishment.
This is the basic form of the word that shows uncertainty or condition

Adding the shadda changes this from a word of uncertainty to a word of certainty. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that the righteous people (لِلْمُتَّقِينَ) will definitely have success (مَفَازًا).
The ت shows this is something you do to yourself - like putting on protection

steps.m: one who is cautious and protects themselves
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to protect) into a noun meaning someone who does the protecting - like changing 'protect' to 'protector'
This makes it plural for masculine nouns

steps.m: for those who protect themselves (from evil/sin)
steps.n: Adds 'for/to' meaning to the word
Changed from basic root meaning 'succeed' into the noun 'success' by adding vowels
Added مَ at the beginning and changed the middle vowel to show where something happens

steps.m: a place of success (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by إِنَّ (indeed/certainly) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed the verb 'surrounded' into a noun meaning 'an enclosed space' by adding ي and ة to make it mean 'garden'
Makes the word plural by adding ا after the second root letter - a common way to make plurals for garden-like words
Basic noun pattern with kasra-fatha vowels
Changes from single grape (عِنَب) to many grapes by adding أ at start and ا after ن
Adds 'and' to connect with the previous word حَدَآئِقَ (gardens)
Changed to show grapes are receiving the action (something being done to them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (he swelled) to describe a person who is swelling/developing (like a young woman)
Changed to plural form to show multiple young women
Added 'and' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this word is being acted upon

steps.m: to be of the same age

steps.n: Basic noun form showing a single peer or age-mate
This is a plural form that changes the word structure to show multiple peers
Adds the accusative case ending to show this word describes كَوَاعِبَ (young women) in the verse
Connected the root letters to form the basic noun. This word is something being given to them in Paradise.
The ًا ending shows this is 'a cup' (not 'the cup') and is receiving the action
The و at the start means 'and'
Forms an adjective describing something that is completely full or overflowing
Shows this word is describing the cup (كَأْسًا) mentioned just before it in the ayah
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) into 'they do not hear'
The shadda (doubled L sound) makes the negation stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' instead of just 'not'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now. The سْ has sukun to show it's silent.

steps.m: they hear/are hearing (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This word appears in a negative context (لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ) meaning 'they will not hear'
This is a preposition that shows something is inside something else
Added ها which refers back to Paradise (الْجَنَّة) mentioned earlier in the surah. This word connects to يَسْمَعُونَ (they hear) to tell us where they don't hear - they don't hear in Paradise.
This root is about empty or meaningless talk
Root letters put together with vowels to make a word meaning 'useless talk'

steps.m: empty/useless talk (object of يَسْمَعُونَ - they hear)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what's being heard (the object)
Basic connecting word that joins this statement to what came before
Adds negation to show the absence of something. Here it negates the word كِذَّابًا (lies) - they won't hear any idle talk AND NOT any lies.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
This form with shadda and alif shows someone who lies a lot

steps.m: a liar (being described in لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is the object (the thing being heard) - so it means they don't hear any liar
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb
Adding اء turns the verb 'reward' into the noun 'reward'

steps.m: a reward/compensation (as the object of عَطَآءً - a gift)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this is the object being given as a gift in the phrase
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it shows the gift is from your Lord.
The letter م gets a shadda (double mark) because it comes after a word ending in ن, making it blend together when read
Root 'r-b-b' becomes 'my Lord' by adding kasra and doubling the 'b' sound
Adding كَ at the end changes 'lord' to 'your lord' (speaking to one male)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
The و changes to اء to make a noun meaning the result of giving

steps.m: a gift (as the object of جَزَآءً/reward)
steps.n: Adding ً shows this word is receiving the action. The آ is just a spelling variation of ا.
Changed the root into a noun meaning the result of counting/calculating. This word describes عَطَآءً (gift/giving) in this ayah.
Added ًا at the end to show this word is receiving the action and matches عَطَآءً (gift/giving) that it describes
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does this action completely, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing

steps.m: Lord of (connected to السَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this word owns or is connected to what comes after it (the heavens). The shadda on ر shows it's connected to the word before it.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
This is how we make it plural when talking about more than one sky/heaven. The word is in genitive case because it is possessed by رَبِّ (Lord/Master), forming what we call a possession relationship.
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and a kasra (ِ) at the end to show it belongs to رَبِّ (Lord)
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to show how to pronounce 'earth'
Added 'the' at the start and the kasra ending shows this earth belongs to the Lord mentioned before it
Added و to connect this with السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it
This is a particle that means 'what' or 'whatever'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Changed the root letters into a word meaning 'between' - used to show the space separating things
Added fatha to show this word connects to other words in the sentence
Added هُمَا at the end to show we're talking about two things (heaven and earth)
Adds ٰن at the end to show someone who is full of mercy
Added definite article ٱل and shadda in one step since they occur together
Added kasra at end because this word follows رَبِّ (Lord of) in the ayah
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess) negative, changing it to 'they do not possess'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Added vowel marks to make it 'he possessed' in the past tense
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to ِ, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: they possess/have power over (referring to those being addressed in لَا يَمْلِكُونَ - they do not have power)
steps.n: Adding ون shows that many people (all male or mixed) are doing the action. In this ayah, it's part of لَا يَمْلِكُونَ meaning 'they do not have power'
This is a preposition meaning 'from'
Adds 'him' (هُ) to show who the word 'from' refers to. Here it refers back to الرَّحْمٰنِ (The Most Merciful) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed the vowel on first letter to kasra and added alif after second letter to make it a noun meaning the act itself

steps.m: any direct speech/address (being acted upon by يَمْلِكُونَ - they possess)
steps.n: Added tanween at the end to show this is indefinite (any speech) and is being acted upon. This word is what's being possessed/controlled in the phrase 'they do not possess/control any direct speech with Him.'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و is a weak letter that takes a sukun.

steps.m: on the Day (when يَقُومُ the Spirit stands)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end to show this word describes when something happens. It's connected to the verb يَقُومُ (stands/will stand) in meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle و causes the first vowel to change to match it (ُ)
Added يَ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, and ُ at end shows he is doing the action. This verb tells us what happens on that Day
Added damma to create the noun form. The و is a weak letter that acts as a long vowel.
Added the definite article ٱل which causes the ر to have shadda. This word is the subject of يَقُومُ (stands) in this ayah.
Changed the root 'possess/rule' into 'one who has special powers' by adding vowels to make the word for angel
Added an alif to make the word stronger, showing something that does an action repeatedly or with strength
Added ال to make it 'the angel' instead of just 'angel'
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the final letter to create the basic noun meaning 'row'

steps.m: in a row/line (describing how the Spirit and angels will stand)
steps.n: Added tanween and alif to show this describes how something is done (adverbial state). This word describes the way the Spirit and angels will stand on that day - arranged in rows.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the negation. This word negates the verb يَتَكَلَّمُونَ (they speak), showing that they will not speak.
Added short vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he spoke'
Doubling the middle letter makes the speaking more intense or repeated
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the person is doing the action for themselves
Adding يَ at the beginning shows the action is happening now or in the future
Adding ونَ at the end shows that many people are doing the action
This is the basic form of the exceptive particle that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before
Added shadda and alif to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows that only those who have permission can speak, excluding them from the general statement that no one will speak.
This is a special word that means 'whoever' or 'the one who'. In this ayah, it refers to whoever is given permission to speak by الرَّحْمٰنُ (the Most Merciful).
Connected the letters with vowels to show this happened in the past. This word shows who is allowed to speak in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to next part

steps.m: to him/for him (the one who has permission from الرَّحْمٰنُ)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه with damma to show 'him'. This refers back to the person who has been given permission by the Most Merciful (الرَّحْمٰنُ) to speak.

steps.m: to show mercy, be kind and compassionate

steps.n: Changes the root into an intensive adjective showing complete/perfect mercy
Added ٱل (the) at beginning and ُ at end to show this is one of Allah's names, referring to The Most Merciful
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern

steps.m: and he said (connecting to what happens when the Spirit and angels stand)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this word to the previous description of the Day when the Spirit and angels stand in ranks
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it into a past tense verb
The verb 'he was correct' became a noun meaning 'correctness' by adding an alif (ا) after the و. This is like changing 'correct' to 'correctness' in English. It's what is being said in the sentence.

steps.m: what is correct/right (object of قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this is what is being said - it's the object of قَالَ (he said). In the ayah, this refers to what the people will say after getting permission from Allah.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above the ذ to show elongation
Adding ل with kasra (ِ) changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end makes it specifically point to something far away
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added ال to make it specific - this refers to a particular day being discussed
The root letters show the basic idea of truth or being right
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to make a noun meaning 'truth'

steps.m: the truth (subject of this part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific ('the truth'), and ُّ at the end shows this is the main subject in this part of the verse
This is a relative pronoun that refers to any person (like 'whoever' or 'one who' in English). It's used to start a conditional phrase here.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This word starts a new phrase and is the subject of the verb شَآءَ (willed/chose) that comes after it.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past
The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a special form
The long alif (آ) shows this is a special type of verb with a weak middle letter

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The ت combines with أ to make a stronger meaning of 'taking'
This is the basic preposition showing direction or destination

steps.m: to, towards (connecting to رَبِّهِۦ - his Lord)
steps.n: Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a common ending for Arabic prepositions. This word connects to رَبِّهِۦ (his Lord) to show where/what direction.
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of nurturing quality
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by or connected to something
Added هِۦ at the end to show this belongs to him. The word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together
Added مَ at beginning to show this is a place where the action happens
Added آ to show returning/going back

steps.m: a place of return (object being described)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being described in the sentence
This is an emphasis particle that makes the statement after it stronger. Here it emphasizes 'We warned you'
The آ at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the next word أَنذَرْنٰكُمْ (we warned you)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb form
Adding أ (hamza) at the start makes it mean 'to make someone warn' instead of just 'to warn'
Added نا at the end to change from 'he warned' to 'we warned' - like saying 'I' vs 'we' in English

steps.m: we warned you all (referring to the disbelievers being warned of punishment)
steps.n: كم shows that multiple people are being warned. This connects to عَذَابًا (punishment) which comes right after in the ayah.

steps.m: to punish severely or cause pain

steps.n: Added ا after second letter to make it a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is what 'we warned you' about in أَنذَرْنٰكُمْ (we warned you).
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to make it indefinite ('a punishment') instead of definite ('the punishment')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective that describes the quality of being near
Added ًا to change from describing a thing as 'near' to describing when something will happen as 'soon'
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with sukun.

steps.m: on the Day (when يَنظُرُ the person will see)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the time when something happens. This connects to the next word يَنظُرُ (will see) - it's saying when this seeing will happen.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he looks/will look (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The يَ at the start shows present/future tense, the ُ marks show it's happening now or in future, and the final ُ shows this is the main doer in the sentence
Root letters م-ر-أ become the noun مَرْء (person) by adding the vowels fatha and sukun. This person is doing the looking in the verse.

steps.m: the person (subject of يَنظُرُ - looks at)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - this refers to a particular person on the Day of Judgment.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the verb يَنظُرُ (he sees) in this ayah. It refers to the things a person will see that they sent ahead (their deeds).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
The shadda makes the meaning more intense and active - showing deliberate sending forward rather than just moving forward

steps.m: she sent forward (what her hands did)
steps.n: The ت at the end shows this is about a feminine subject (she/it). In this ayah, this refers to what someone's hands sent forward (their deeds)
Root letters put together to make the word 'hand'
Adding ا at the end makes it mean 'two' of something

steps.m: his two hands (subject of قَدَّمَتْ - what he sent forth)
steps.n: Added هُ to show these are his hands. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb قَدَّمَتْ (sent forth/put forward) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا changes back to و.
Adding و at the start connects this word to what came before it
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning

steps.m: one who disbelieves (subject of يَقُولُ - says)
steps.n: This pattern shows someone who does the action. The word الكافر is the subject (doer) of the verb يقول (says).
ال makes it definite, ُ shows it's the subject
This is a particle used to express a wish or regret about something impossible or unlikely
Added يٰ at the beginning to show strong emotion or regret
Added نِى at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. This word expresses the disbeliever's regret in the ayah, wishing they were dust instead of facing judgment.
The middle weak letter و drops out and the vowels adjust according to Arabic rules for hollow verbs
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. This word is part of what the disbeliever says in the ayah.
Added ُ (damma) on first letter and ا (alif) after second letter to make it into a noun meaning the substance itself
Added ًا at the end to show this is what 'I was' in the phrase 'I wish I was dust'
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation that means the same thing
